A previously unpublished photograph from the Epstein case files shows **Prince Andrew** seated with **an unidentified woman on his lap** during what appears to be a social gathering, according to court documents released this week.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

The image, which surfaced as part of ongoing document releases related to Jeffrey Epstein's criminal cases, adds to the collection of photographs documenting the Duke of York's social connections to Epstein and his circle.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> The woman in the photograph has not been publicly identified in the court filings.<sup>[2](#ref-2)</sup>

## Context of Release

The photograph emerged amid continued releases of documents related to Epstein's sex trafficking operation and subsequent legal proceedings.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> The image appears in materials that have been unsealed as part of civil litigation connected to the case.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

Prince Andrew, who was stripped of his royal titles and military affiliations in January 2022, has previously denied allegations of sexual misconduct connected to Epstein's criminal enterprise.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> In February 2022, he settled a civil lawsuit brought by **Virginia Giuffre**, who alleged she was trafficked to him by Epstein when she was 17.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

## Previous Photographic Evidence

The newly released image joins other photographs that have documented Andrew's association with Epstein and his associates.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> The most widely circulated photograph shows Andrew with his arm around Giuffre's waist, with **Ghislaine Maxwell** standing beside them, though Andrew has questioned the photograph's authenticity.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

Details about the specific location or date of the newly released photograph have not been disclosed in available court documents.<sup>[2](#ref-2)</sup> The identity of the woman seated on Andrew's lap remains unknown in public filings.<sup>[2](#ref-2)</sup>

## Legal Background

Epstein was convicted in 2008 on state charges of soliciting a minor and served 13 months in a work-release program.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> He was arrested again in July 2019 on federal sex trafficking charges and died by suicide in his jail cell one month later.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

Maxwell, Epstein's longtime associate, was convicted in December 2021 on federal charges including sex trafficking of minors and is currently serving a 20-year prison sentence.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

The continued release of court documents has provided additional details about Epstein's social network and the operation of his trafficking enterprise, though many questions about the full scope of his connections remain unresolved.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>
